summary: Matched the previously-assembly `ft_8009665C` routine as `ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput` in a new ftCommon source/header pair, removing the function body and related constants from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`. The new C implementation checks B-button aerial special input, dispatches to per-fighter `ftData_SpecialAirHi/Lw/S/N` callbacks based on stick direction and thresholds, handles facing/turnaround edge cases, and sets the fighter flag at `x2227_flag.bits.b5` after successful dispatch. Call sites in common IASA states and character files were updated to include `ftCo_SpecialAir.h` and call the named function. The build was updated to compile `ftCo_SpecialAir.c`, and supporting headers gained the needed ftdata declarations and a typed `ftCommonData::x224` field. The PR report shows `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_SpecialAir.c` at 100.00% with score 0/15500.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_SpecialAir.c` with a matching implementation of `ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput(ftCo_GObj* gobj)`.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_SpecialAir.h` with include guard `GALE01_09614C` and the `/* 09614C */ bool ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput(ftCo_GObj* gobj);` prototype.
Removed the old `ft_8009665C` assembly body from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` and replaced assembly call labels with `ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput` at many IASA call sites.
Updated C callers in `ftCo_DamageFall.c`, `ftLk_SpecialS.c`, and `ftPe_Float.c` from `ft_8009665C` to `ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput`, adding the new header include where needed.
Removed the `ft_8009665C` prototype from `ftCo_0961.h`, leaving that header to start at `ft_800968C8` and changing its include guard from `GALE01_09614C` to `GALE01_0963B8`.
Added `ftData_SpecialAirS` and `ftData_SpecialAirN` declarations to `src/melee/ft/ftdata.h` and changed the baselib include from `<baselib/gobj.h>` to `<baselib/forward.h>`.
Typed `ftCommonData` offset `+224` as `int x224`, shifting the following padding member to `x228`, so the matched C can compare `fp->x676_x < p_ftCommonData->x224`.
Updated `src/melee/ft/ft_0877.dox` to use the new `ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput` name.
Added `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_SpecialAir.c.o` to `obj_files.mk` before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o` object.
smart_moves: The function was extracted into a focused `ftCo_SpecialAir` module rather than kept in the broad numeric `ftCo_0961` header, making future special-air input work easier to find.

The old address-based symbol `ft_8009665C` was replaced at all visible call sites with the behavior-oriented name `ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput`, clarifying that this routine is an input gate/dispatcher.
Per-fighter special-air action tables were modeled as `HSD_GObjEvent` arrays in `ftdata.h`, letting the C mirror the assembly's indexed callback dispatch through `fp->kind`.
The implementation preserved early returns on missing callback table entries, matching the assembly's null checks before calling each special-air handler.

The code uses existing field/flag names such as `fp->input.lstick`, `fp->facing_dir`, `fp->x2227_flag.bits.b5`, and `fp->x2228_flag.b7` instead of raw byte operations, improving readability while still matching.
The PR included the build-system object addition, avoiding a common asm-to-C migration failure where the new C file exists but is not linked into the text object list.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling an input dispatcher, name it for the high-level decision it performs (`CheckInput`) and move it near the related ftCommon action family instead of leaving it in a numeric catch-all header.
Function-pointer tables should be declared in shared data headers as soon as matched C needs direct indexed calls; this avoids local extern hacks and documents the table's real type.
A successful match may require small struct typing improvements, such as converting an opaque padding range into `int x224` when assembly comparisons show a scalar integer field.
Do not forget non-C references: assembly labels, Doxygen declarations, and source callers all needed the new name to keep the repository searchable and consistent.

The final C structure can be simpler than the assembly branch layout while preserving behavior: check B input first, test vertical thresholds before horizontal threshold, then fall through to neutral special.
Header ownership matters: removing the old prototype from `ftCo_0961.h` and placing it in `ftCo_SpecialAir.h` prevents the numeric header from continuing to claim an address range it no longer owns.
naming_conventions: The new module follows the ftCommon file naming pattern: `ftCo_SpecialAir.c` and `ftCo_SpecialAir.h`.
The new header include guard uses the function address-style guard `GALE01_09614C`; after moving that address to the new header, `ftCo_0961.h` starts at `GALE01_0963B8`.
Per-direction special-air tables use the pattern `ftData_SpecialAirHi`, `ftData_SpecialAirLw`, `ftData_SpecialAirS`, and `ftData_SpecialAirN`.
The matched function uses `ftCo_GObj*` and `ftCo_Fighter*` naming in the ftCommon context.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The assembly's `fp->input.x668 & HSD_PAD_B` gate was expressed directly as the top-level condition; if absent, the function returns `false`.
Vertical special selection matches signed stick thresholds: `lstick.y >= p_ftCommonData->x21C` for up special and `lstick.y <= -p_ftCommonData->x21C` for down special.
Horizontal special selection uses `ABS(fp->input.lstick.x) >= p_ftCommonData->x218`, matching the assembly's absolute-value branch sequence.
The side-special turnaround support was represented as `fp->input.lstick.x * fp->facing_dir < -p_ftCommonData->x220` followed by `ftCommon_8007D9FC(fp)`.
Neutral special preserves the assembly's early facing flip when `fp->x676_x < p_ftCommonData->x224` and `facing_dir` disagrees with `x2228_flag.b7`.
Each successful callback dispatch sets `fp->x2227_flag.bits.b5 = true` and returns `true`; missing callback entries return `false`.
Constants formerly emitted in `ft_08A4.s` as `.L_804D85C0`, `.L_804D85C4`, and `.L_804D85C8` became ordinary C uses of `0`, `-1`, and `+1`/boolean-facing comparisons.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The only human/body evidence included a match report showing `ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput` at 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further name `ftCommonData` fields `x218`, `x21C`, `x220`, or `x224` used by special-move input thresholds.
Find PRs that decompile the per-fighter `ftData_SpecialAir*` callback tables or rename their entries.
Search for remaining `ftCo_0961.h` numeric prototypes after `ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput` was split out.
Search for other functions calling `ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput` to confirm all aerial IASA states use the named header.
Look for later cleanups of `ftCo_SpecialAir_CheckInput(void)` in `ft_0877.dox`, because the dox excerpt shows a no-argument declaration despite the header taking `ftCo_GObj*`.
